crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Leasable area of commercial office REITs to rise 25-30% by fiscal 2028
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The leasable area of domestic commercial office real estate investment trusts (REITs)  will increase by 40-45 million square feet (msf) to 190–195 msf by the end of next fiscal, driven by planned asset additions by the existing REITs and the recent listing of a new REIT.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Guardrails bolster microfinance securitised pool performance
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The collection performance of microfinance securitised pools  has improved sharply after the implementation of guardrails on new loan originations.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: PVC pipe makers to see revenues grow 10-15% on higher realisations
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Organised polyvinyl chloride (PVC) pipe and fitting makers are expected to see their revenue surge by 10-15% this fiscal as higher crude oil prices—following the conflict in West Asia—have shored up realisations. However, sales volume is likely to moderate because of lower demand in some segments. I

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Indigenous solar cells to meet ~half of demand after ALCM mandate
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Indigenous solar cells are expected to account for ~half of the overall demand this fiscal, up from a fourth last fiscal. This strong uptick will be driven by the government’s push to reduce dependence on imports, supported by a strong ramp-up in solar cell manufacturing capacity. However, such larg

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Operating profit of domestic airlines to fall 10-15% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The operating profit  of domestic airlines  will decline 10-15% this fiscal due to elevated aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices, airspace restrictions and rupee depreciation amid the West Asia conflict.

crisil-research.title: SectorVector - Pivoting the pipelines
crisil-research.description: Recent months have seen a sharp realignment in India’s liquified petroleum gas (LPG) imports amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.

crisil-research.title: US private credit trends, 1Q26 - from resilience to risk awareness
crisil-research.description: The first quarter of 2026 marked a key inflection point for US private credit, as the asset class shifted from being viewed as broadly insulated to facing a more nuanced mix of vulnerabilities and opportunities, with heightened scrutiny around liquidity, valuations, PIK usage, and retail-led redempt

crisil-research.title: Giving diligence its due
crisil-research.description: The ascent of alternatives in the past few years has been significant both in terms of size and breadth. By 2030, with more than 2,500 registered funds in the market, alternative investment funds (AIFs) are projected to account for more than $425 billion, or 9%, of the managed funds in India.

crisil-research.title: SectorVector - Hot streak
crisil-research.description: A severe and prolonged heatwave drove a surge in usage of cooling equipment, boosting power demand across the country.

crisil-research.title: Crisil Economy First Cut: Sharp upside
crisil-research.description: India’s merchandise exports saw a broad-based 18% acceleration on-year to $45.2 billion in May, compared with 13.8% to $43.6 billion in April. Merchandise imports surged 20.6% on-year to $73.4 billion after 10.0% growth to $71.9 billion in April.

crisil-research.title: Indian AI's three tests
crisil-research.description: India's AI sector attracted more capital in the first half of 2026 than in all of 2025, driven almost entirely by two deals: Neysa Networks ($600 million) and Sarvam AI ($234 million Series B).

ssga-insights.title: What could revive 2026 Fed cut prospects?
ssga-insights.description: Improving labor data and firm inflation keep the Fed on hold, but cooling jobs, softer demand, eased oil risks, or market stress could revive 2026 rate cuts.

ssga-insights.title: Equities overweight edges higher
ssga-insights.description: We modestly increase our equities overweight exposure to 4.0% from 2.5% before, funded by a reduction in US aggregate bonds.

ssga-insights.title: Core-Satellite Fixed Income: Evolving from concept to practice
ssga-insights.description: A Core-Satellite approach to fixed income is increasingly being pursued—see how 3 institutional investors applied this approach to help achieve their objectives

ssga-insights.title: US inflation may be nearing its peak
ssga-insights.description: Inflation remains elevated but may be peaking, while global central banks diverge on policy as growth, sentiment, and inflation outlooks stay uncertain.

ssga-insights.title: The future of fixed income: From income to engineered outcomes
ssga-insights.description: Read more about the new, modern fixed income. The asset class is increasingly a strategic allocation for building targeted exposures and solutions.

ssga-insights.title: Emerging Market Debt Commentary: May 2026
ssga-insights.description: EM debt pushed higher in May, with hard currency leading the way as spreads tightened and local markets navigated a firmer dollar and global uncertainty.

ssga-insights.title: India loses EM share as AI trade surges
ssga-insights.description: India’s weight in the MSCI Emerging Markets Index has declined sharply, while Taiwan and Korea gain amid capital shifts toward AI-driven semiconductor markets.

ssga-insights.title: Case Study: An institutional allocator enhances its core-plus fixed income allocation by capturing private credit exposure with an ETF
ssga-insights.description: Case Study: An institutional allocator enhances a core-plus fixed income allocation by capturing private credit exposure with an ETF.

ssga-insights.title: Introducing Trump Accounts: How to jumpstart investing for your children
ssga-insights.description: Learn how Trump Accounts work: a new child-focused investment account designed to support early, tax-advantaged, long-term investing.

ssga-insights.title: Growth takes lead in FX markets
ssga-insights.description: Geopolitics and growth divergence reshape FX markets, as investors refocus on growth, inflation, and policy, driving clearer differentiation across currencies.

ssga-insights.title: Mega-cap IPOs: Implications for institutional investors and index managers
ssga-insights.description: A potential wave of mega-cap IPOs in 2026, including large venture-backed firms, could reshape global and US equity benchmarks.

ssga-insights.title: Investing in AI through sectors
ssga-insights.description: Learn how AI is showing up across different sectors, the investment opportunities, and the implications for investors as they construct portfolios to target AI growth.

ssga-insights.title: A smarter core: An Enhanced approach to optimizing equity portfolios
ssga-insights.description: Is there a more thoughtful way to anchor your portfolio and deliver ballast—while improving the efficiency of returns beyond the traditional satellite approach? Explore an Enhanced approach to optimizing equity portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: Gold 2026 Midyear Outlook: A tug-of-war between tactical and structural momentum
ssga-insights.description: Structural support for gold remains intact, with prices potentially reaching $5,500/oz by year-end. Record government debt, elevated stock-bond correlations, strong physical demand, and low fund ownership underpin the outlook, though energy-driven inflation and hawkish Fed and G10 policy pose near-t

ssga-insights.title: India government bonds get a glow-up
ssga-insights.description: India has removed withholding and capital gains taxes on government bonds for foreign investors, improving access, potential returns and support for the rupee.

ssga-insights.title: Why investors should reconsider APAC weight
ssga-insights.description: APAC equities remain underweight in portfolios despite rising economic significance, offering diversification benefits and exposure to global growth drivers.

ssga-insights.title: Implementation alpha across the fixed income spectrum
ssga-insights.description: Disciplined implementation can reduce costs drag and capture inefficiencies across the fixed income spectrum from indexing to enhanced and active strategies.

ssga-insights.title: US labor strength delays Fed rate cuts
ssga-insights.description: Strong US labor data delays Fed cuts, Canada job strength supports BoC hold, ECB set to hike despite weak growth and mixed global economic signals.

ssga-insights.title: The value of dividend growth strategies to portfolios today and beyond
ssga-insights.description: In times of uncertainty, pivoting away from concentration and leveraging the stability of a dividend growth strategy with SDY can potentially help investors reduce the impact of volatility.

ssga-insights.title: Why Asia may be the biggest winner of the global AI boom
ssga-insights.description: Asia anchors the global AI ecosystem through semiconductors and cost-efficient deployment, with divergent labor outcomes and proactive tax policies shaping growth.

ssga-insights.title: Support levels in sovereign debt markets
ssga-insights.description: Is the rise in major sovereign bond yields a temporary response to geopolitical tensions, or are markets settling into a new regime of higher rates?

ssga-insights.title: Emerging market debt: Why it belongs in your investment portfolio
ssga-insights.description: No longer a niche exposure, emerging market debt has become a key allocation that offers income, diversification, and a growing role in investment portfolios.

ssga-insights.title: ETF inflows set records as leadership narrows
ssga-insights.description: Record ETF flows highlight concentrated equity positioning and strong fixed income demand as investors navigate resilient earnings and rising macro uncertainty.

icici-bank-research.title: Morning Market Starter
icici-bank-research.description: India’s wholesale price inflation (WPI) accelerated to a 42-month high of 8.3% YoY in April from 3.88% YoY in March, driven by a sharp rise in crude oil, natural gas, food and fuel prices alongside a favourable base effect. Read the report to know more.

ing-think-rss.title: National Bank of Hungary review: ‘Mini’ cutting cycle set to size up
ing-think-rss.description: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: The writing was on the wall, and the National Bank of Hungary did not disappoint. On the contrary, the revised ultra-low inflation outlook encouraged the Monetary Council to commit to two more rate cuts after today’s reduction during the summer. We envisage at least one a

ing-think-rss.title: Eurozone activity remains lacklustre as price pressures ease
ing-think-rss.description: The composite PMI increased from 48.5 to 49.5 in June. This still marks sluggish economic activity for the bloc, but the easing of price pressures indicated by the survey is encouraging. The combination of sluggish growth and fading inflation concerns marks a dovish reading for the ECB

ing-think-rss.title: French economic outlook remains downbeat
ing-think-rss.description: EUROPE: Business sentiment stayed subdued in June and domestic demand continues to underperform, pointing to weak economic activity in the months ahead

ing-think-rss.title: Rates Spark: Gilts don’t like political uncertainty
ing-think-rss.description: Mixed signals from Christine Lagarde are suggesting a more balanced view from the ECB, potentially with more focus on growth after new PMI data. Meanwhile, UK gilt yields are likely to keep a material risk premium as we head into a period of economic policy uncertainty

ing-think-rss.title: FX Daily: Focus remains on yen and sterling
ing-think-rss.description: The options market appears to be pricing in a greater chance of imminent JPY FX intervention ahead of the US-Japan finance ministers' meeting. In the UK, sterling rallied on hopes that market-friendly Wes Streeting will become the Chancellor in a new government led by Andy Burnham. The dollar remai

ing-think-rss.title: The Commodities Feed: Oil sells off as US eases sanctions on Iran
ing-think-rss.description: Oil prices weakened as the US announced a temporary sanctions waiver on Iranian oil exports

ing-think-rss.title: Aluminium deficit persists despite easing Middle East tensions
ing-think-rss.description: Geopolitical tensions have eased, reducing upside risks for aluminium prices, but market balances remain tight and the deficit persists

ing-think-rss.title: Polish activity data slows further in 2Q but 3.4% growth still achievable for 2026
ing-think-rss.description: POLAND: May retail sales and construction output disappointed, and GDP growth in the second quarter is set to fall below the 3.5% YoY rate seen in the first quarter. Despite softer consumption, strong investment should keep 2026 GDP growth at 3.4%. Wages show no second-round energy effects, allowing

ing-think-rss.title: FX Daily: Lack of GBP political premium cuts both ways
ing-think-rss.description: Sterling shows little sign of stress despite the prospect of an imminent change in prime minister. This points to stronger market trust in Burnham, but also leaves the currency more exposed if fiscal concerns resurface. Elsewhere, Fedspeak and Strait of Hormuz news should drive most FX moves this w

ing-think-rss.title: ING lifts South Korea 2026 GDP growth forecast to 4% from 3%
ing-think-rss.description: ASIA/PACIFIC: Under ING’s latest energy scenario, supply conditions should improve for South Korea in the second half of 2026. Semiconductor momentum, meanwhile, will remain stronger than previously assumed. This is supporting private consumption and investment, on top of government spending. We’ve

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why Spain’s Economy Is Growing Three Times Faster Than the Euro Area
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Spain’s strong fiscal position and ‘structural resilience’ have bolstered GDP at a time when its neighbors are struggling to cope with the global energy shock.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The S&P 500 Is Forecast to Climb as Earnings Growth Powers Stocks Higher
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Goldman Sachs Research raised its S&P 500 year-end target to 8000, projecting earnings per share of $340 in 2026 (a 24% increase, year over year). AI infrastructure investment is expected to account for about half of the earnings growth.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Investors May Be Overlooking the UK’s Economic Potential
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Europe] The UK economy is beset by weak growth, soft productivity gains, persistent inflation, and pressure on fiscal policy. However the UK retains important strengths and has opportunities in the medium-term to boost economic growth, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for India’s Stock Market Amid the Middle East Conflict
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Markets] Indian equities have historically rebounded after periods of crisis, which may offer attractive entry points for long-term investors in the markets.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: The Outlook for Data Centers in Asia
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] Traditional cloud workloads as well as new AI-first demand are driving growth in data centers in Asia, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Private Markets Are Expected to Have a Growing Role in Data Center Financing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Artificial Intelligence] With planned hyperscaler spending on AI and data centers projected to top $5 trillion by 2030, capital from private market infrastructure and real estate funds will become increasingly important for the AI buildout.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why Oil Prices Could ‘Grind Lower’ Amid the US-Iran Deal
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Energy] While the recent deal between the US and Iran is likely already reflected in the oil market, prices are likely to gradually decline over time, says Jerome Dortmans, co-head of Global Oil and Products Trading in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets.

goldman-sachs-reports.title: Why the Fed Is Unlikely to Cut Rates This Year
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Macroeconomics] Goldman Sachs Research has pushed back its forecast for Fed rate cuts to June and December 2027 (from December 2026 and March 2027 previously).

goldman-sachs-reports.title: UK Social Mobility: Remains a Tough Climb but One Worth Pursuing
goldman-sachs-reports.description: [Europe] Increasing social mobility, an important objective in its own right, would also likely have economic benefits, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

jpmorgan-research.title: US–Israel military operation against Iran: Are markets on edge?
jpmorgan-research.description: The conflict could pose an upside risk for oil, gold and aluminum prices — though uncertainties around its longer-term impact remain.
